Investor Presentation SEPLAT ENERGY November 2025

Important Notice Disclaimer . Any forward-looking statements reflect the Company's current view with respect to future events and are subject to risks relating to future events and other risks, uncertainties and assumptions relating to the Company's business, results of operations, financial position, liquidity, prospects, growth, strategies and the oil and gas business. Forward-looking statements speak only as of the date they are made and cannot be relied upon as a guide to future performance. The Company undertakes no obligation to update any forward-looking statements, whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise, except to the extent legally required. No part of these results constitutes, or shall be taken to constitute, an invitation or inducement to invest in the Company and must not be relied upon in any way in connection with any investment decision. Investor Presentation - November 2025

Seplat Energy at a Glance Pure play Nigerian independent upstream E&P Investor Presentation - November 2025 2P reserves (31 Dec 2024) 1,043 MMboe 9M 25 WI production 135.6 Kboepd 3Q 25 dividend (+63% QoQ) 7.5 US c/shr Dual listed in Lagos and London SEPLAT: NGX SEPL: LSE 99% Nigerian ~1,500 employees Market Cap (30th Oct 2025) $2.5 Billion Net Debt (30th Sept 2025) $0.4 Billion Credit Profile S&P: B, Fitch: B (u/g April '25), Moody's: B2 (u/g June '25) NIGERIA'S LEADING INDEPENDENT ENERGY COMPANY

9M 2025 Highlights

9M 2025 Corporate Highlights Strong performance in the period and enhanced scale Investor Presentation - November 2025 Adj. EBITDA $1.1 billion Credit Ratings upgrades WI production 135.6 kboepd Group revenue $2.2 billion Adjusted EBITDA

+190% YoY, 51% margin

9M25 CFFO (pre-tax): $1,395.4 million (9M 2024: $357.8 million) Credit ratings agency upgrades in 1H 25

Moody's (up to B2 from Caa1), one notch inside Sovereign

Fitch (up to B from B-)

External recognition of strong asset base and balance sheet Working Interest Production

Up 185% YoY. 78% Oil & NGL, 22% Gas

Delivered upper half of 2025 guidance (120-140 kboepd) Reported Revenue

Up 204% YoY

94% Oil & NGL, 6% Gas (9M 2024: 87%:13%)

3Q 2025 Corporate Highlights Delivering enhanced value for shareholders and Nigeria Investor Presentation - November 2025 Revenue Diversification Quarterly dividend 7.5 US cents/share EORF Projects CO2 emissions -34% kgCO2/boe (onshore) LPG sales commenced from offshore operations

First Liquefied Petroleum Gas ('LPG') cargo sold to the domestic market, improving energy access and supporting clean cooking 3Q 25 dividend

Core - 5.0 US cents per share, Special - 2.5 US cents per share

+108% YoY on Q3 2024, +63% QoQ on 2Q 2025 dividends Significant progress made in EORF ambitions

Oben LPG project and SIGP LPG module completed in 3Q 2025

Ohaji flares out project reached mechanical completion Sustained reduction in Scope 1 CO2 emissions intensity

3Q 25: 22.0 kg CO2/boe (1Q 25: 30.7 kg CO2/boe, 2Q 25: 23.2 kg CO2/boe)

Impact of Sapele AG compressor, +ve for emissions and opex

Operational Performance

39,763 11,142 - 77,562 47,525 135,636 9M 2024 9M 2025 OMLs 4, 38, 41 OML 40 OML 53 OPL 283 OMLs 67, 68,70,104 OML 99 (A/K Field) Total Upstream Operational Performance Solid operational performance underpins growth trajectory Investor Presentation - November 2025 Positive results from Offshore Idle well programme

3Q 2025, we completed workover activities on a further 4 wells. YTD total 33 wells worked-over

Of the 33 wells, 26 are producing, adding gross JV production capacity of 33.4 kbopd (W.I: 13.4 kbopd).

Progress on the program will accelerate in Q4 2025 PRODUCTION QoQ (boepd): Steady production performance across our assets in 3Q 2025 UNIT PRODUCTION OPEX ($/boe): Production costs were higher in the quarter; reflects asset integrity costs & O&M 2025 IDLE WELL PROGRAMME (kbopd): Cumulative gross JV production restored YTD of 33.4 kbopd PRODUCTION YoY (boepd): 9M 2025 production rose 185% reflecting step change in scale of the business ONSHORE CO2 EMISSIONS INTENSITY (kgCO2/boe): Aided by commencement of SIGP commercial operations 2.9x IDLE WELL PROGRAMME 39,010 12,779 79,379 49,268 47,558 45,768 69,033 131,674 137,207 137,889 Q1 2024 Q2 2024 Q3 2024 Q4 2024 Q1 2025 Q2 2025 Q3 2025 OMLs 4, 38, 41 OML 40 OML 53 OPL 283 OMLs 67, 68,70,104 OML 99 (A/K Field) 31.09 31.79 33.17 32.29 30.65 23.17 22.00 Q1 2024 Q2 2024 Q3 2024 Q4 2024 Q1 2025 Q2 2025 Q3 2025 1.63 2.46 8.62 3.73 9.55 9.86 10.89 18.54 12.59 12.49 16.99 Q1 2024 Q2 2024 Q3 2024 Q4 2024 Q1 2025 Q2 2025 Q3 2025 Crude Handling Charge Barging Production Personnel Costs Core Operations & Maintenance Other Field Expenses* Total Production Opex 33.4 0.0 5.0 10.0 15.0 20.0 25.0 30.0 35.0 40.0 Jan Feb Mar Apr May Jun Jul Aug Sep

Midstream Gas Operational Performance Gas growth picking steam as critical projects near completion Investor Presentation - November 2025 GAS PRODUCTION QoQ (MMscfd): -8% GAS PRODUCITON YoY (MMscfd): +69% REALISED GAS PRICE YoY ($/Mscf) - Realised gas price continues to remain robust across assets ANOH GAS PROJECT SAPELE GAS PLANT

3Q 2025, plant received third party gas to commence live hydrocarbon testing.

Commercial negotiations for offtake via the alternative route (Nigeria LNG) have been concluded. Sufficient demand to operate at nameplate capacity.

First gas targeted in 4Q 2025.

1Q 2025: Reliability test for 30 MMscfd MRU completed and LTO obtained from NMDPRA.

2Q 2025: The second MRU, a 60 MMscfd facility, is now operational in line with guidance.

3Q 2025: Completed the 60 MMscfd reliability testing. Commissioned the Sapele gas plant LPG module and phase 1 of the LPG storage & loading facility

Aim to complete the final two phases of the LPG storage and loading facility in 4Q 2025. LPG commercial operations to commence in 4Q 2025. 132.5 30.3 12.0 103.6 174.7 9M 2024 9M 2025 OMLs 4, 38, 41 OMLs 67, 68,70,104 OML 99 (A/K Field) 109.5 107.9 93.6 134.6 166.2 186.2 171.7 Q1 2024 Q2 2024 Q3 2024 Q4 2024 Q1 2025 Q2 2025 Q3 2025 OMLs 4, 38, 41 OMLs 67, 68,70,104 OML 99 (A/K Field) 2.87 2.85 2.82 2.9 3.08 3.13 2.38 1.93 2025 YTD Onshore realised gas price Offshore realised gas price Blended realised gas price

The project is designed to double the existing pipeline capacity from 120 MMscfd to 240 MMscfd.

In 3Q 2025, detailed engineering design was progressed to approximately 75% completion.

Onshore fabrication will commence in 1Q 2026.

Timeline for project completion remains 2026 which will double gas sales capacity at Oso-BRT. OSO-BRT GAS PLANT UPGRADE

31.1 31.8 33.2 32.3 30.6 23.0 22.0 Q1'24 Q2'24 Q3'24 Q4'24 Q1'25 Q2'25 Q3'25E Q4'25E 2026E 9M 2025 HSE Update Onshore operations on track to end routine flaring in 2025 Investor Presentation - November 2025 2025e 2026e 2027e 2028e 2029e 2030e Onshore Consolidated Emission Intensity (kg CO2 per boe) - Operated Assets Offshore Consolidated Emission Intensity (kg CO2 per boe) Onshore Positive impact of End of Routine Flaring projects clearly seen in 2Q and 3Q 2025 25% reduction in emissions intensity Completion of all onshore EoRF projects by end 2025 On track for emissions intensity to fall below <15 kg CO2/boe in 2026+ LTI recorded in 3Q 2025 at Oben. Offshore Planning for flare reduction in progress Work programme to continue through 2025/26 to commit forward path Yoho production platform suffered a fire incident during the quarter. Impact to 4Q 2025 production estimated at 10 - 12 kbopd. No LTI recorded on offshore operations in the period.

Financial Performance

9M 2025 Financial Highlights Step change in scale reflected in financial performance Investor Presentation - November 2025 Lower realised prices

Realised Oil price $71.93/bbl (9M24, $82.89/bbl)

Realised Gas price $2.95/Mscf (9M24, $3.18/Mscf) due to lower offshore gas pricing Strong production & liftings

Oil volumes lifted 3.7x YoY: - 9M 2025 = 27.9 MMbbls - 9M 2024 = 7.5 MMbbls

Gas sales volume of 46.3 Bscf (9M 2024: 28.4 Bscf)

NGLs volumes lifted of 734.9 kbopd Strengthened balance sheet

Net debt down 57% since YE 2024 Dividend growth

$7.5c/shr, +63% vs. 2Q 2025, +108% vs. 3Q 2024 Improving credit ratings

In June 2025, Moody's upgraded our credit rating to B2 (stable), from Caa1 (positive), one notch ahead of the sovereign rating.

April 2025, Fitch upgraded to B from BGROUP REVENUES $2,177m (9M 2024, $715m) ADJ. EBITDA $1,112m(1) (9M 2024, $383m) (1) EBITDA adjustments include adjusting for non-cash items such as impairment, fair value, and exchange losses DIVIDEND $7.5c/shr (3Q 2024, $3.6c/shr) NET INCOME $95m (9M 2024, $35m) 2.9x 3.0x 2.1x 2.7x POST-TAX CFFO $1,011m (9M 2024, $358m) NET DEBT $386m (YE 2024, $898m) 2.8x 57%

9M 2025 Financial Results Strong revenue and cashflow growth aided by robust operations despite weaker prices Investor Presentation - November 2025 $ million 9M 2025 9M 2024 Change Notes Realised oil price ($/bbl) 71.93 82.89 (13%) Global macro concerns (demand) weigh on oil price Oil revenue 2,004.4 625.2 221% Higher oil liftings following SEPNU consolidation. Higher production. Gas revenue 139.2 90.2 54% Higher production from Oben, commencement of operations from SIGP, and SEPNU consolidation NGL revenue 33.0 - nm 735 kbbls of NGLs sold Total revenue 2,176.6 715.3 204% Cost of sales (1,297.2) (360.3) 260% Higher production, maintenance costs for the offshore assets, DD&A Gross profit 879.5 148% G&A (169.8) (95.9) 77% Reflects larger organisation. 9M in-line with budget Underlift 3.8 8.2 (53%) Produced barrels not sold as of period end. Other Income 22.7 13.2 72% Other non-core income such as FX gains, tariff income, asset disposal etc. Operating profit 711.0 274.8 159% Net finance costs (137.1) (50.3) 173% Impact of higher gross debt post SEPNU acquisition, commitment fees on Refinancing on $650m bond Profit before tax 570.1 245.0 133% Tax expense (475.0) (209.7) 126% Reflects consolidated tax position of the group. Improving ETR at 83% Profit/(Loss) for the period 95.1 35.3 170% Capital investment 180.1 157.0 15% Capex ramp up on onshore drilling and offshore IGE replacement Net cash generated from operations 1,010.8 357.8 183% Increased liftings during the period, improved working capital conditions

9M 2025 Cashflow Waterfall Strong cashflow generation aided by higher liftings and improving cost efficiencies Investor Presentation - November 2025 1,395 (329) (3) (52) (180) (20) (64) (95) (170) (380) FY 2024 closing cash balance Cashflow from operations Tax & hedge premium Restricted cash Contribution to plan assets Capital expenditure JV investment Payment to ExxonMobil Other investing cashflow Dividends paid Financing and others Cash before debt movements Net debt movemnts 6M 2025 closing cash balance Annual w/c adjusted FCF generation ($m) Operating FCF +$831m Strong cashflow growth CFFO $1,395 million: +230% YoY due to higher liftings and positive receivables collection Pro-active and prudent management of cash balances to repay debt FCF $831 million: +314% YoY, high quality, cash generative production Adjusted for working capital impact, FCF was $585 million Funding of SEPNU pension liabilities Additional investment in AGPC towards completion Q4 2024 DPS - US$3.6c Special DPS - US$3.3c Q1 2025 - US$4.6c Q2 2025 - US$4.6c 9M 2025 *FCF adjusted for working capital impacts

Strong and Fortified Balance Sheet Improved balance sheet with conservative leverage and flexible liquidity Investor Presentation - November 2025 GROSS CASH $580m(1) (YE24, $470m) GROSS DEBT $966m(2) (YE24, $1,368m) NET DEBT $386m (YE24, $898m) (1) Gross cash excludes $135m of restricted cash (2) Gross debt includes an adjustment for accrued interest (3) Pro-forma 2024 EBITDA calculated as: $440.0 million onshore, $913.5 million offshore. PF 9M 2025 EBITDA calculated as $401.0 million onshore, $1,019.7 million offshore Balance sheet strength maintained over time Leverage: <1x since acquisition completion $m Pro-Forma YE24 Pro-Forma 9M 25 Net Debt TTM EBITDA 1,354(3) 1,421(3) ND/EBITDA (x) 0.66x 0.27x *Proforma Net Debt-to-EBITDA Liquidity: Sufficient flexibility to support growth Debt profile: No near-term maturities Cash Balance Undrawn RCF Total Liquidity Advance Payment Facility Eurobond Notes Drawn Eland RBL (Senior) 1.0 0.8 1.0 0.9 0.7 1.2 0.9 0.7 0.9 0.8 0.5 0.7 0.6 0.5 0.3 3.0 2.0 Net Debt to EBITDA Debt Covenant Corporate Policy

New Dividend Policy Raising our commitment to shareholders Investor Presentation - November 2025 FIXED FOR CERTAINTY VARIABLE WITH BUSINESS PERFORMANCE 2026-2030 CUMULATIVE CASH DIVIDEND FCF LINKED DISTRIBUTION TARGET OUTCOME MINIMUM ANNUAL DIVIDEND AT LEAST 40% THROUGH THE CYCLE (2026-2030) ~$1.0 BILLION $120 MILLION THROUGH CYCLE DISTRIBUTION 40%-50% FCF MATERIAL IN SCALE (equivalent to ~$1.66/SHR) PROVIDES VISIBILITY, A SECURE YIELD (equivalent to 20C/SHR PER ANNUM)

Rewarding Shareholders Strong operating performance underpinned improved 3Q 2025 dividend Investor Presentation - November 2025 Material $7.5c/shr declared for 3Q 25 $5.0c/shr base dividend in line with new dividend policy, plus $2.5c/shr. special dividend Special dividend payment underlines strong cashflow generation in 2025 YTD $762m cumulative cash dividends paid to shareholders by end 9M 2025. Equivalent to 142% of equity raised from shareholders (all at IPO) Consistent Dividend paid in 11 out of past 12 years. Opportunity to pay special twice a year (Feb, Oct) Secure Minimum dividend assured down to $50/bbl oil price. 0.10 0.10 0.15 0.15 0.17 0.17 2025 YTD Q1 Q2 Q3 Q4 Special NEW ANNUAL MINIMUM DIVIDEND ~20c/shr per annum ~5c/shr per quarter - 2025 YTD Total ... Total equity ... CMD: Implementing the new dividend policy (Annual dividend per share) Consistent: Cumulative Cash Dividends vs Equity Issuance ($m, only raised equity at IPO)

Onshore: new well stock, first gas at ANOH

PIA conversion approval for onshore operated assets Investor Presentation - November 2025 Production (kboepd) 135.6 130-140 120-140 Capex ($m) 270-290 260-320 Operating Costs ($/boe) 14.1 14.0-15.0 unch Updated 2025 Guidance 9M 2025 Actual Prior 2025 Guidance

Roadmap Highlights

Investment Case & Key Takeaways Transformational acquisition sets us on a path of growth and enhanced shareholder returns Investor Presentation - November 2025 A giant resource base supports our ability to grow production and cash flow for enhanced shareholder returns Dividend security, with significant upside potential Giant Resource Base 2.3 BN Organic Production Growth ~200 kboepd 2P + 2C (Gross JV ~5.5 BN boe) 2030 target (Gross JV ~500 kboepd) We operate in one of the world's premier hydrocarbon provinces

Roadmap 2030: Total Shareholder Return Giant resource base underpins production growth and increased distributions to shareholders Investor Presentation - November 2025 TARGET CUMULATIVE CASH DIVIDEND TARGET CUMULATIVE OPERATING CASH FLOW TARGET WORKING INTEREST PRODUCTION GROW EARN RETURN ~200 KBOEPD $5.0-$6.0 BILLION ~$1.0 BILLION (equiv. $1.66/share) >50% vs. 135 kboepd at 6M >150% vs. 2020-2024 ($0.39 BN) >150% vs. 2020-2024 ($2.0BN)

Giant Reserves Base Working interest reserves life of 21 years* underpins our growth ambitions Investor Presentation - November 2025 BALANCED PORTFOLIO OF RESERVES WEIGHTED TO OFFSHORE GAS SIGNIFICANT SCALE TO UNDERPIN GROWTH 1,043 MMBOE (prev. 886 MMboe) 1,262 MMBOE (prev. 330 MMboe) 2,305 MMBOE (prev. 1,216 MMboe) 47% ONSHORE 53% OFFSHORE 7% ONSHORE 93% OFFSHORE 25% ONSHORE 75% OFFSHORE 65% LIQUIDS 32% GAS 3% NGL 32% LIQUIDS 51% GAS 17% NGL 47% LIQUIDS 42% GAS 11% NGL 2P RESERVES 2C RESOURCES 2P + 2C * Reserves life (R/P ratio) calculated as 2P reserves divided by annualized 6M 2025 WI production

Improving Domestic Macro Environment The oil & gas sector has rebounded strongly as economic conditions in Nigeria have improved Investor Presentation - November 2025 INFLATION RATE (%): Headline inflation rate has resumed a downward trend due to exchange rate stability, tight monetary policy, and improving supply chain dynamics NGN/USD Exchange Rate: NGN has recorded more stability in the last 12 months as tight monetary policy has encouraged increased FPI flows in the FX market OIL SECTOR GDP (%): Nigeria's oil sector continues to stage strong rebound from 2020/21 lows Source: National Bureau of Statistics (NBS) Source: Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN), Bloomberg Source: National Bureau of Statistics (NBS) 1% 5% -28% -39% 25% 4% 6% 1,000 1,200 1,400 1,600 1,800 Jul-23 Aug-23 Sep-23 Oct-23 Nov-23 Dec-23 Jan-24 Feb-24 Mar-24 Apr-24 May-24 Jun-24 Jul-24 Aug-24 Sep-24 Oct-24 Nov-24 Dec-24 Jan-25 Feb-25 Mar-25 Apr-25 May-25 Jun-25 Jul-25 Aug-25 Sep-25 Oct-25 10% 15% 20% 25% 30% 35% 40% Jan-20 Apr-20 Jul-20 Oct-20 Jan-21 Apr-21 Jul-21 Oct-21 Jan-22 Apr-22 Jul-22 Oct-22 Jan-23 Apr-23 Jul-23 Oct-23 Jan-24 Apr-24 Jul-24 Oct-24 Jan-25 Apr-25 Jul-25

Key Operational Workstreams to 2030 Bigger and better diversified business Investor Presentation - November 2025 Onshore Offshore Development Drilling (Oil & Gas Wells) Development Drilling (Oil & Gas Wells) Idle Wells Restoration Programme Oso-BRT Phase 1 Oso-BRT Phase 2 2031 - 2035 Yoho Gas Development Additional Gas developments Jisike Buffer Tanks Amukpe Storage Tanks Onshore Flowstation/Facilities Upgrade Sapele LTF & Gaslift Compression Station

Gas Projects Unlock Sales Capacity Four gas plant projects to be completed by 2030, doubling group gas sales capacity Investor Presentation - November 2025 PROJECTS LIST OSO-BRT Gas Plant Oso Base Plant Capacity Oso-BRT Phase 1 2025e 2026e 2027e 2028e 2029e 2030e 700 MMscfd capacity target 855 MMscfd capacity target PROJECTED ONSHORE GAS SALES CAPACITY PROJECTED OFFSHORE GAS SALES CAPACITY Oben Gas Plant ANOH Gas Plant Sapele Integrated Gas Plant Oso-BRT Phase 1 Yoho Gas Development Oso Base Plant Capacity PROJECTED JV GAS PRODUCTION 2025e 2026e 2027e 2028e 2029e 2030e Onshore Gas ANOH Offshore Gas Significant gas capacity supports production growth Onshore production to reach capacity with ANOH start up Offshore project development underway 2025e 2026e 2027e 2028e 2029e 2030e

Capital Allocation Framework Enlarged company to deliver a material increase in cash generation Investor Presentation - November 2025 DIVIDEND CAPEX & ABEX ~$5.0-6.0bn at $65/bbl, $39/bbl NGL and $2.75/mscf DEBT SERVICE SURPLUS CASH BALANCE SHEET, ASSET GROWTH & INCREASING DISTRIBUTIONS ~$2.0bn at $75/bbl and $2.8/mscf ~2.5x growth Estimated CFFO & Capital Allocation Outlook 2026-2030 (5yr) Historical CFFO & Capital Allocation 2020-2024 (5yr)

Capital Expenditure Guidance Capex expected to increase as work programme accelerates Investor Presentation - November 2025 2025E 2026E 2027E 2028E 2029E 2030E Capex Future Capex $2.5-3.0 billion 30% 70% Onshore Offshore 63% 37% Wells Facilities & Other INVESTMENT BY BUSINESS % INVESTMENT BY TECHNICAL ACTIVITY % CAPEX history & outlook - $m Capex Ambitions: Deliver production growth targets, focused on: Drilling Gas Infrastructure Preparing for post 2030 growth Deliver 2P reserves and develop large 2C resource opportunity Support tax efficient operations, by building capital allowances

Cost Optimisation Focus on optimisation, efficiency and value capture to drive unit opex back below $10/boe Investor Presentation - November 2025 0.0 4.0 8.0 12.0 16.0 2024 2025E 2026E 2027E 2028E 2029E 2030E At 6M25 FY25 Guide Target UNIT PRODUCTION OPEX $12.5/boe $14-15/boe <$10/boe Production Opex per boe outlook Significant benefits emerging from new scale

Enhanced negotiating position with local supply chain will aid strategic contract renegotiations

Cost optimization from larger work programme

Leverage technology Asset efficiency and optimisation

Asset integrity and O&M expenses aimed to improve asset operational safety, efficiency, and reliability.

Execute non-oil & gas asset rationalisation Business plan production opex guidance

Plan to reduce production opex to below $10/boe by 2030

Tax Guidance Fiscal levers to enhance the benefit of investment in the offshore Investor Presentation - November 2025 6M 2025 FY 2025 ETR outlook (% of PBT) 91% 80-85%* Estimate <60% 6M 2025 FY 2025 Cash tax outlook (% CFFO) 28% <50% Estimate <40% Tax Efficiency Levers Ramp up capex investment in our assets to grow production and build adequate capital allowances to improve tax shield. This delivers the twin impact of higher JV production and after-tax cashflows Delivery of our gas projects on our offshore assets will lead to more diversified cashflows which will be taxed at 30% CIT, adding lowertaxed cashflows to our group after-tax operating cashflows. Conversion of licenses from the Petroleum Profit Tax (PPT) regime to the PIA regime. Our current guidance is to achieve onshore assets conversion by 2026 and offshore assets conversion by 2027. Capital Expenditure Investing in Offshore Gas Petroleum Industry Act * Updated at 9M 2025 results
